questions.question: The abbreviation for refrigerants containing chlorine, fluorine, and carbon is ____.
A refrigerant reclaimed from a system may be charged back into the system from which it came.
The DOT-approved cylinder in which refrigerant may be recovered has a mechanism for shutting the recovery system off when it reaches approximately ____% full.
What is tropospheric ozone and why are we not concerned with protecting it?
options: It is not a pollutant. It dissipates on its own.
It is a pollutant and may irritate mucous membranes when inhaled (known as smog).
questions.correct_answer: It is a pollutant and may irritate mucous membranes when inhaled (known as smog).

questions.question: Production of CFC refrigerants became illegal on January 1, ____.
When did it become illegal to intentionally vent HFC refrigerants to the atmosphere?
How are near-azeotropic blends identified?
How many chlorine atoms does it take to destroy 100,000 ozone molecules in the stratosphere?
The DOT-approved cylinder used for recovering refrigerant is gray and yellow.
Which of the following refrigerants is a CFC-type refrigerant?
The abbreviation for refrigerants containing fluorine, carbon, and hydrogen is ____.
The abbreviation for refrigerants containing chlorine, fluorine, carbon, and hydrogen is ____.
On what does Type III EPA certification allow a service technician to work?
options: Low-pressure appliance using refrigerants below 0 psig at 100°F
Low-pressure appliance using refrigerants below 30 psig at 104°F
questions.correct_answer: Low-pressure appliance using refrigerants below 30 psig at 104°F

questions.question: Refrigerants that are made up of two or more liquids are called ____.
The fine for venting refrigerant into the atmosphere can be as high as $____ per day.
When a technician works for a company in which the technician does not have a financial interest, the technician does not have to be certified.
On what does Type I EPA certification allow a service technician to work?
options: Equipment containing five pounds or less of refrigerant
Equipment containing ten pounds or less
Equipment containing fifteen pounds or less
Equipment containing twenty pounds or less
questions.correct_answer: Equipment containing five pounds or less of refrigerant

questions.question: Why is stratospheric ozone so important to our environment?
To remove the refrigerant from a system without any testing or processing and to be put back into the same system is called ____.
To clean refrigerant by the use of filter dryers is called ____.
Refrigerants that have a low ODP will have a high TEWI.
Which type of refrigerant has the highest Ozone Depletion Potential (ODP)?
Mechanical refrigerant recovery equipment must meet specifications established by the 1993 AHRI 740 standard.
Ozone is produced mainly by a process called ____.
The _____ takes into consideration both the direct and indirect global warming effects of refrigerants.
Sludge is produced when moisture, acid, and ____ are present in the system.
Liquid water under the oil in a motor-compressor can be removed under deep vacuum by running the compressor briefly.
A pressure of 100 microns is reached, pump is turned off, gauge rises to 500 microns and stays. What does this indicate?
Why must all valves be opened during evacuation?
A micron gauge is used to measure ____.
options: High pressure in a refrigeration system

questions.question: A pressure of 100 microns is reached, pump off, gauge rises above 5,000 microns and keeps rising. What does this indicate?
Air entering a refrigerant system can eventually lead to compressor failure.
Only two substances should be in the refrigeration system for proper operation. They are ____ and oil.
The maximum recommended pressure for leak testing is 150 psig on the low-pressure side.
When moisture is present in a refrigeration system, it reacts with refrigerant and oil to form ____.
When using a standing pressure test, ____ should never be used.
A multiple evacuation means ____.
options: Vacuum more than once with nitrogen or refrigerant between
questions.correct_answer: Vacuum more than once with nitrogen or refrigerant between

questions.question: Why is nitrogen considered a noncondensable gas?
options: It will not condense in the condenser
questions.correct_answer: It will not condense in the condenser

questions.question: A compound gauge ____.
options: Is one of two gauges on a manifold

questions.question: The oil in a vacuum pump should be changed after each use.
Is it a good idea to remove Schrader stems before evacuation?
options: Yes, they restrict flow and increase evacuation time
questions.correct_answer: Yes, they restrict flow and increase evacuation time

questions.question: Ordinary clean refrigerant oil can be used satisfactorily in a vacuum pump.
A rising pressure could also mean refrigerant vapor is still seeping from the system.
The electronic vacuum gauge measures pressure ____ the system.
A pressure of 100 microns is reached, pump off, gauge rises to 5,000 microns and stays. What does this indicate?
Moisture in liquid state must be ____ before removal with a vacuum pump.
After a compressor burnout, it is good practice to reuse the recovered refrigerant.
Triple evacuations are not normally performed on large systems because ____.
options: Large systems only need one evacuation

questions.question: A deep vacuum involves reducing pressure to ____ microns or below.
An electronic charging scale counts upward as refrigerant is removed to show how much was used.
If a critically charged system is overcharged, what is a likely result?
When charging a system with a near-azeotropic mixture, what must be done to prevent liquid from entering the system?
options: Charge vapor through the high side
Charge vapor through the low side using a resistor
Charge liquid through the high side
Charge liquid through the low side using a restrictor
questions.correct_answer: Charge liquid through the low side using a restrictor

questions.question: Refrigerant in liquid form is normally put into the system through the ____.
What is the correct refrigerant charge for a system with the given factory charge and line lengths?
When adding refrigerant in vapor form to an operating system, it must be added to the low-pressure side.
Fractionation occurs when ____.
options: Only a fraction of the charge has been added
Some of a blended refrigerant evaporates or condenses before the rest
questions.correct_answer: Some of a blended refrigerant evaporates or condenses before the rest

questions.question: When a warm refrigerant cylinder is connected to an operating system, the cylinder pressure will be ____ than the suction pressure.
Refrigerant in vapor form is normally put into the system through the ____.
If 22 oz of refrigerant are added and the cylinder originally weighs 24 lb 12 oz, what will it weigh afterward?
The quickest method to charge a system is to charge only vapor.
How can a technician increase cylinder pressure when it becomes too low to charge?
options: Place tank in warm water (90°F or less)
correct_answer: Place tank in warm water (90°F or less)

questions.question: If the tank is reusable, system refrigerant can enter the tank if system pressure is higher.
A graduated cylinder charging device ____.
options: has a heater to prevent temperature drop

questions.question: As refrigerant vapor is removed from a supply cylinder, the pressure will ____.
Using a restrictor, liquid refrigerant can be charged into the low side with the system operating.
When system pressures are lower than tank pressure, refrigerant can be added to both high and low sides.
A manufacturer's charging chart may be used to determine correct charge.
Glide is the range of temperatures/pressures in which a blended refrigerant will condense.
When adding liquid refrigerant into the high side, the compressor must be running.
Charging a refrigeration system refers to ____.
Refrigerant vapor is added to an operating system through the ____ side.
When charging liquid refrigerant from a single-valve cylinder, the cylinder is placed ____.
If 30 oz of refrigerant are added and the cylinder originally weighs 26 lb 4 oz, what will it weigh afterward?
The ____ is used to take high-side pressure readings and trap refrigerant in the receiver.
There are three kinds of attic ventilation: power, natural, and ____.
A homeowner building a house in Las Vegas should locate most windows on the ____ sides to reduce solar load.
The package air-conditioning system is ____ than the split system because it is completely factory assembled and charged.
When exposed to salt air, ____ should be used for screws securing sheet metal on air conditioning equipment.
The supply duct portion of an air handling system needs insulation when run through an unconditioned space.
Appearance is a major drawback with what type of air-conditioning equipment?
On a package air-conditioning system, which refrigerant lines must be field-installed?
options: No lines need to be field-installed
questions.correct_answer: No lines need to be field-installed

questions.question: The return duct portion of an air handling system never needs to be insulated.
What is the major disadvantage of a top-air-discharge condenser?
options: More difficult to install and maintain
Fan and motor are more subject to damage from rain, snow, and falling leaves
questions.correct_answer: Fan and motor are more subject to damage from rain, snow, and falling leaves

questions.question: When an 'A' coil is used horizontally, the coil is laid on its side and a special ____ pan is required.
What are the three common evaporator designs used on air conditioning equipment?
More than one evaporator coil circuit is used when ____.
options: A slant coil is at an angle more than 60°
The evaporator is outside the conditioned area
There would be excessive refrigerant pressure drop in the coil circuit
A distributor would not be practical
questions.correct_answer: There would be excessive refrigerant pressure drop in the coil circuit

questions.question: The solar load on a structure is the heat gain resulting from ____.
